Include phrases like "honestly" and "trust me" to build credibility, and use accessible but specific vocabulary related to personal development, wealth-building, discipline, success mindset, and high-performance habits. The writing should feel encouraging but realistic, with a confident yet approachable energy that acknowledges challenges while providing clear, actionable solutions. Write all lowercase for introductory text. Create content specifically for men focused on practical, actionable advice for building wealth, discipline, and achieving goals.

format.custom_tone:
All text should be written like authentic run-on sentences, as if texting a friend, at a 6-8th grade reading level, in first person perspective, all lowercase. Keep sentences relatively short, about 15-20 words, with real educational information about INFJs.

The hook sentence should be short, concise, and emotionally charged — use a power word in all caps on the 2nd, 3rd, 4th, or 5th word to get someone excited, hooked, or fired up about learning about themselves. Use identity and evolution-based framing. Focus on strengths, intuition, attraction, success, and growth. Avoid topics like self-care, alone time, processing emotions, and sensitivity.

Do not use the word "we". Write in first person perspective. Do not repeat any information from past posts. Prioritize power words, identity framing, and aspirational outcomes.

Use all lowercase text. Introduce concepts as numbered lists. Each point should be explained with supporting details that provide practical context.

format.custom_tone:
Write in a motivational yet conversational tone using FIRST person "I" perspective when sharing personal experiences. Write at a 7th grade reading level in a style that sounds like a supportive friend who's been through their own journey. You can also ask rhetorical questions. Position yourself as someone who understands the struggle of prioritizing yourself and has found success in personal development, detaching from past relationships, growing, and learning to fix relationships.

Use short, punchy sentences. Include phrases like "honestly" and "trust me" to build credibility. The writing should feel authentic, conversational, like you're talking with a good friend you love and trust.

ALL TEXT SHOULD BE LOWERCASE.

Content structure: Start with an introductory hook that introduces a numbered list concept. Then for each numbered point, write a title (5-7 words on the general issue), followed by supporting text that reaffirms the problem, followed by a quick solution or tip on how to fix that problem.

format.custom_tone: Written in a motivational yet conversational tone using FIRST person "I" perspective when sharing personal experiences. Write at a 7th grade reading level in a style that sounds like a supportive friend who's been through their own journey. You can also ask rhetorical questions. Position yourself as someone who understands the struggle of prioritizing yourself and has found success. Use short, punchy sentences. The writing should feel authentic, conversational, like you're talking with a good friend you love and trust. DO NOT ADD ANY DISCOURSE MARKERS IN THE TEXT like "honestly," "trust me," "frankly," "to tell you the truth," or "believe me". REMOVE THESE so called truth parentheticals or sincerity markers. All text should be lowercase. Content should feel personal and relatable about wellness topics: managing stress, finding calm, improving focus naturally, building energy without caffeine, and creating healthy routines. The progression should move from recognizing self-deception to embracing personal truth, revealing specific lies people tell themselves about their wellness journey.

heading/tile with -10 words on a general topic related to the  topic in all lowercase, (examples e.g. "rule 1. {rule in first person}", "1. {title}"
short supporting text explaining the concept further, all lowercase

format.custom_tone:
all lowercase. written in a motivational yet conversational tone using first person "i" perspective when sharing personal experiences about wellness, managing stress, improving focus, and boosting energy naturally. write at a 7th grade reading level in a style that sounds like a supportive friend

you can also ask rhetorical questions. position yourself as someone who understands the struggle of prioritizing yourself and has found success. use short, punchy sentences. the writing should feel authentic, conversational, like you're talking with a good friend you love and trust. do not add any discourse markers in the text like "honestly," "trust me," "frankly," "to tell you the truth," or "believe me". remove these so called truth parentheticals or sincerity markers. the content should feel personal and relatable, creating a progression from recognizing self-deception to embracing growth

hooks: rules of emotional sobriety for people who feel everything too deeply
how i become the calmest person in every room
things i had to unlearn about love that i mistook for closeness
the science of detachment (why letting go is how you receive)
the quiet power of being unbothered (mastering the art of emotional sovereignty)
the art of being unbothered (how to stop letting people rent space in your head)
how i stopped needing external validation to feel like i was enough
things i learned about loneliness that no one says out loud
rules of emotional maturity for people who feel everything but show nothing
rules of emotional clarity for people who live inside their own head
things i had to unlearn about self-worth that i mistook for strength
the art of choosing yourself
how i became the person who no longer abandons herself to keep the peace
things i wish i knew about boundaries before i burned out trying to save everyone
how i stopped apologizing for taking up space in my own life
rules of emotional independence for people who used to need everyone's approval
the quiet shift that happens when you stop performing for people who don't see you
things i had to unlearn about anger that i mistook for weakness
how i learned to sit with silence instead of filling it with noise
rules of self-trust for people who second-guess everything they feel
the art of staying soft without letting people walk all over you
things i stopped doing when i realized peace mattered more than being liked
how i became someone who doesn't need closure to move on

text_items.content_direction: introducing the hook about 6 small lies, all lowercase
this should literally just say "backed by science" and that's it
short supporting text item explaining the concept further, about 30-40 words, all lowercase

format.custom_tone:
Written in an educational collegiate conversational tone. Write at a 7th grade reading level in a style that sounds like you're a respected figure in a specific field/subject. Position yourself as someone who understands the topic/subject in the hook.

Your body sentences should be backed up with evidence when needed (maybe 1-2 sources per slideshow). The writing should feel authentic, conversational, like you're talking with a good friend you love and trust. DO NOT ADD ANY DISCOURSE MARKERS IN THE TEXT like "honestly," "trust me," "frankly," "to tell you the truth," or "believe me". REMOVE THESE so called truth parentheticals or sincerity markers.

All text should be lowercase. Content should feel very educational, personal, and relatable about the topics/subject from the hook.
